I've been doing art for sale to the public since 1999.
I've been a published illustrator since 2007.
I've been selling print-on-demand items featuring my art through my own stores since 2010.
I've needed generative AI before. Why would I suddenly need it now?
I've no use for it.

For those people generative AI can function as an "inspiration mill" not really different from mood books or inspirational collections.
AI (so far) can't come up with anything "new" so there will remain a need for people in the loop. 
And just taking the raw output from gen AI is not art as craft.

So, if you want to have discourse on if generative AI is a tool artists can use, I'd say no. Because you aren't using a tool to make art yourself. You're commissioning a piece of "art" from a non-thinking machine who spits out the common denominator based on the prompt.
